title: Nozzle for dispensing viscous material
abstract: The present invention relates to nozzles that are suitable for dispensing of viscous materials such as sealants or bonding materials from tubes cartridges, or other containers. More specifically the invention relates to nozzle tips from which sealants or similar viscous materials may be discharged and applied in a smooth and consistent manner to allow for easy application and a consistent end product. The nozzle has a body portion and a dispensing head disposed at the distal end of the body portion. The dispensing head has an open ended cavity bounded by at least one cavity wall and having surface contacting edges, the cavity being in fluid communication with a conduit in the body portion.
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . A nozzle for dispensing viscous fluid such as bonding or sealant material onto a surface, comprising; a body portion having a conduit therethrough, the conduit configured to provide a fluid flow path, said body portion having a base section extending from the lower distal edge thereof which, in use, contacts the surface; and a dispensing head disposed at the distal end of the body portion, wherein said dispensing head has an open-ended cavity bounded by at least one cavity wall and having surface contacting edges, the cavity being in fluid communication with the conduit of the body portion and wherein the at least one cavity wall of the dispensing head extends out further distally than the base section such that when viscous fluid is dispensed it will be shaped by the cavity wall as it is discharged. 2. A nozzle as in Claim 1 wherein the cross-sectional area of the cavity is greater than the cross section area of at least a distal portion or end of the conduit. 3. A nozzle as in Claims 1 or 2 wherein the cavity is 30%-50% wider than the distal portion or end of the conduit. 4. A nozzle as in any of the previous Claims wherein the surface contacting edges are substantially parallel. 5. A nozzle as in any of the previous Claims wherein the dispensing head is provided with flanges that extend outwards from the surface contacting edges. 6. A nozzle as in any of the previous Claims wherein the viscous fluid is a sealant such as a silicone, a glazing compound, a bonding compound, a filler or a caulking compound. 7. A nozzle as in any of the previous Claims wherein the surface contacting edges extend out further distally than the base section. 8. A nozzle as in any of the previous Claims wherein the base section is planar or flat. 9. A nozzle as in Claim 8 wherein the base section comprises a guide flange on its lower surface. 10. A nozzle as in Claim 9 wherein the guide flange is an elongate linear guide flange. 1 1 . A nozzle as in any of Claims 1 to 7 wherein, the base section is V shaped. 12. A nozzle as in Claim 1 1 wherein the internal angle of the V is 90Â°. 13. A nozzle as in any of Claims 1 to 7 wherein the base section has an inverted V shape. 14. A nozzle as in any of the previous Claims wherein the nozzle is provided with attachment means for attaching it to a viscous fluid dispensing device. 15. A nozzle as in Claim 18 wherein the attachment means is one or more male or female members suitable for attaching to appropriate male or female members. 16. A nozzle as in any of Claims 14 or 15 wherein the attachment means allows for a push-fit. 17. A nozzle as in any of Claims 14 or 15 wherein the attachment means is a screw thread. 18. A nozzle as in any of Claims 14 to 17 wherein the attachment means is adapted to allow only a single use of the nozzle. 19. A nozzle as in any of the previous Claims wherein the nozzle is provided with an attachment sleeve at the proximal end of the body portion, having a conduit 

 configured to provide a fluid flow path and be in fluid communication with the fluid flow path of the body portion when in use. 20. A nozzle as in any of the previous Claims wherein the attachment sleeve is either removably attached to the body or permanently attached to the body. 21 . A viscous fluid dispensing device comprising a nozzle according to any of Claims 1 to 20. 22. A viscous fluid dispensing device as in Claim 21 wherein the nozzle is removably attachable to the dispensing device. 23. A viscous fluid dispensing device as in Claim 21 wherein the nozzle is permanently attached to the dispensing device. 24. A viscous fluid dispensing device as in Claim 21 wherein the nozzle is single use and attachable to the dispensing device for one use only.
